title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Nail Fungus
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a natural and effective way to treat nail fungus. These oils are extracted from plants and are known for their antifungal properties. For example, Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass oil, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail infections. Similarly, Eucalyptus globulus oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with nail fungus. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a well-known antifungal agent that can help to eliminate fungal infections.  When you use herbal essential oils for nail fungus, you can relieve the symptoms of the infection, such as discoloration, thickening, and brittleness of the nails. These oils can also help to promote healthy nail growth and prevent future infections. To use these oils, you can add a few drops to a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and apply it directly to the affected nail. You can also mix it with a herbal tea, like peppermint or chamomile, to create a soothing foot soak.  Herbal teas can also be a great way to relieve nail fungus, as they can help to promote healthy nail growth and prevent future infections. The antifungal properties of these teas can help to eliminate fungal infections, and the antioxidants can help to promote healthy nail growth. For example, a tea made from the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant can be used as a foot soak to help relieve nail fungus.  Using herbal essential oils and teas for nail fungus can bring many benefits to your life. It can help you to avoid the use of harsh chemicals and antibiotics, which can have negative side effects. It can also help you to promote healthy nail growth and prevent future infections. Additionally, the antifungal properties of these oils and teas can help to prevent the spread of fungal infections to other parts of the body.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for nail fungus

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains limonene, geranial, and neral, which are its active constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help combat nail fungus. The antifungal properties of limonene and geranial prevent the growth of fungi that cause nail infections, such as Trichophyton and Aspergillus. The antimicrobial properties of neral also help reduce the risk of infection and promote a healthy environment for nail growth. By inhibiting the growth of fungi and promoting a healthy environment, Cymbopogon citratus essential oils can help treat nail fungus and promote healthy nails.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems. Wash them with 1 cup of water to clean.
Use a blender to crush the leaves and stems into a pulp. You need 1/2 cup of the pulp.
Transfer the pulp to a saucepan with 1 cup of water. Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Let the mixture sit for 2 days. This allows the essential oil to separate from the water. Skim the oil and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains a compound called cineole, which has antifungal properties that help combat nail fungus. Cineole works by penetrating the nail and treating the fungal infection from the inside out. The antiseptic properties of eucalyptus globulus essential oils also help to reduce the risk of infection and promote a healthy environment for nail growth. The antimicrobial properties of the oil, particularly the presence of globulol, help to eliminate fungal spores and prevent their return. Regular application of eucalyptus globulus essential oils can help to treat nail fungus by killing the fungus and promoting healthy nail growth.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of water.
Put the Eucalyptus leaves in a large pot and pour in the water.
Heat the mixture on low heat for 2 hours, then turn off the heat.
Let the mixture cool and then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Store the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have antimicrobial properties. These properties help to combat fungal infections that cause nail fungus, such as Trichophyton rubrum and Candida albicans. Cineole helps to penetrate the nail plate and reach the infected area, while terpinen-4-ol has antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi. The antimicrobial properties of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils also help to prevent the spread of infection and promote a healthy environment for nail growth. Regular application of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils to the affected nails may help to reduce fungal growth and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, 2 cups of water, and a large saucepan.
Combine the leaves and water in the saucepan, and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30-40 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Use a hydrosol or a carrier oil to dilute the essential oil to 5-10% concentration, and store in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are responsible for its antifungal properties. These constituents help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus. The oil's antimicrobial properties also help to reduce the spread of fungal infections and promote a healthy environment for nail growth. Additionally, the terpenes present in Lavandula angustifolia essential oils help to reduce inflammation and promote a balanced pH, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth. By using Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, you can help to create a healthy environment that promotes the growth of strong and fungus-free nails.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Combine the flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the solids.
Add 10-15 drops of vitamin E oil to the liquid and stir well. This helps preserve the oil.
Bottle the Lavandula angustifolia essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops of the oil per application to help treat nail fungus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains compounds like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which have antifungal properties. These properties help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, such as Trichophyton rubrum and Candida albicans. The essential oil's antimicrobial properties also help to reduce the risk of infection and promote a healthy environment for the nails to grow. The antiseptic properties of borneol and bornyl acetate in the oil help to kill fungal spores and prevent them from spreading. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils topically, you can create an environment that is less conducive to the growth of nail fungus.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water.
Combine the leaves and water in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to a still or a double boiler. Heat the liquid to 212°F (100°C) for 2-3 hours.
Allow the liquid to cool and separate into an oil and water phase. Skim off the oil and transfer it to a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils contains thymol, carvacrol, and linalool as its main bioactive constituents. Thymol and carvacrol have antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections, while linalool has antifungal properties that target the growth of fungi. The antimicrobial and antifungal properties of these constituents help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, such as Trichophyton rubrum. Thymol and carvacrol also have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with nail fungus. By applying Thymus vulgaris essential oils to the affected area, the bioactive constituents can work together to help treat and manage nail fungus.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris leaves and 2 cups of water. Clean a glass jar with a lid.
Add the Thymus vulgaris leaves to the glass jar and pour the water over them. Cover the jar with a lid.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2-3 hours or overnight. This will help the leaves release their oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids.
Store the extracted oil in a dark glass bottle and use it to treat nail fungus by applying a few drops directly to the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon nardus essential oils contains compounds like linalool and geraniol, which have antifungal properties. These compounds help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus. The antiseptic and antifungal properties of Cymbopogon nardus essential oils help to treat nail fungus by reducing the amount of fungal spores on the nail. The antifungal compounds in Cymbopogon nardus essential oils, such as linalool, also help to break down the cell walls of fungi, making it difficult for them to survive. The antimicrobial properties of Cymbopogon nardus essential oils also help to prevent the spread of fungal infections.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon nardus herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cymbopogon nardus leaves, 1 cup of water, and a distillation apparatus or a pot with a steamer.
Wash the Cymbopogon nardus leaves with 1 cup of water to remove dirt.
Place the cleaned Cymbopogon nardus leaves in the distillation apparatus or the steamer pot.
Steam the Cymbopogon nardus leaves with 1 cup of water for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the extracted essential oil and filter it using a coffee filter to remove any impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum camphora essential oils contains cineole, camphene, and linalool as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds give the oil antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat nail fungus. Cineole has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells, while camphene has strong decongestant and antiseptic properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection. Linalool also has antifungal properties that aid in killing fungal spores and preventing their growth. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of Cinnamomum camphora essential oils make it a potential treatment for nail fungus.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum camphora herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cinnamomum camphora leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Steep the Cinnamomum camphora leaves in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the mixture every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the leaves.
Transfer the oil mixture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil per application.
Apply the oil to affected nails 2-3 times a day, massaging it gently into the nail and surrounding skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album essential oils contains santalol and alpha-santalol, its main active constituents. These compounds have antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help combat nail fungus. Santalol has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, making it effective in treating fungal infections. The antifungal properties of Santalum album essential oils can help break down and eliminate fungal spores, promoting healthy nail growth. By using Santalum album essential oils, individuals can potentially alleviate the symptoms of nail fungus and achieve healthier nails.
remedies.image_alt: Santalum album herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Santalum album leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a coffee grinder, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Grind 1/2 cup of dried Santalum album leaves into a fine powder using the coffee grinder.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the ground leaves with 4 tablespoons of carrier oil in the glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil infuse.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and use the infused oil for nail fungus treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and linalool. These bioactive constituents have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Cinnamaldehyde is particularly effective against fungal growth, inhibiting the development of nail fungus. Eugenol also helps to combat fungal infections by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment around the nail. The antifungal properties of these constituents in Cinnamomum verum essential oils make it a potentially effective treatment for nail fungus.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinnamomum verum leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Crush the Cinnamomum verum leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the crushed leaves with the carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar daily.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the Cinnamomum verum essential oil to a dark glass bottle and use it as needed to treat nail fungus. Store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils contains boswellic acids, including akkadasin, boswellic acid A, and boswellic acid B. These boswellic acids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with nail fungus. The antimicrobial properties of boswellic acids also inhibit the growth of fungi, such as Trichophyton rubrum, that cause nail fungus. Additionally, boswellic acids have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce pain, which can help to promote healthy nail growth and prevent further infection. By applying Boswellia serrata essential oils to the affected nail, the boswellic acids can penetrate deep into the nail plate and nail bed to target and eliminate the fungal infection.
remedies.image_alt: Boswellia serrata herbal essential oils for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 100g of Boswellia serrata resin, a cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), and a dark glass bottle.
Melt the Boswellia serrata resin in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until the resin turns into a liquid.
Add 50ml of carrier oil to the melted resin and stir well. Continue heating for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle.
Store the Boswellia serrata essential oil in the dark glass bottle and use it for nail fungus treatment by mixing 2-3 drops with a carrier oil and applying it to the affected area.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent nail fungus from forming, but it's not a guarantee. Some herbal teas, like tea tree oil and oregano tea, have antifungal properties that could help keep your nails healthy. These teas might be able to inhibit the growth of fungus, but more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to your nail fungus treatment, but it's essential to consume them in moderation. Drinking them daily can lead to an overload of certain herbs, which may cause more harm than good. It's best to start with a small dose and adjust as needed to ensure a safe and effective treatment.
faq_timeline: The time it takes for herbal teas to show results in nail fungus varies. Some people may notice changes in a few weeks, while others might not see improvements for several months. It's common for nail fungus to take a long time to clear up completely, but herbal teas can help speed up the process.
faq_time: For nail fungus, try drinking herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. These times can help your body absorb the active ingredients, which may aid in fighting the fungus. Drinking tea too close to bedtime might make it harder to sleep due to the caffeine or other stimulating compounds present in some herbal teas.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for nail fungus, but it's not well understood. Some herbal teas, like those containing berberine, may have similar effects to certain medications, like terbinafine. This could lead to changes in how the medications work or increase the risk of side effects. If you're taking prescription medication, talk to your doctor about any herbal teas you're using.
faq_pregnant: It's not recommended to consume herbal teas for nail fungus while pregnant. Some herbal teas may have unknown effects on the baby, and the risk of potential harm outweighs any possible benefits. Stick to safer, more established treatments for nail fungus, and choose herbal teas only after you've given birth.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and want to try herbal teas for nail fungus, be cautious. Some herbal teas, like tea tree oil and oregano, can pass into breast milk and might affect your baby. Also, their effects on nail fungus aren't proven. It's best to talk to your body about how it reacts to these teas, and consider their potential impact on your baby.
title_2: 11 Herbal Essential Oils to Aid Runners with Nail Fungus Problems
